title: rubus × latibracteatus
intro: Rubus × latibracteatus, also known as the Himalayan Raspberry or Himalayan Raspberry Bramble, is a hybrid of the Himalayan raspberry (Rubus fortunei) and the bramble (Rubus spp.). The plant contains anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other bioactive compounds that provide its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Rubus × latibracteatus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and certain types of cancer. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in jams and jellies, and is also used as a food ingredient in some Asian cuisines.
